sections.recommendation:
Adopt a resolution:
1. Approving the transfer of the Managing General Partner role for Golden Bear
Homes, LP from Bay Area Community Services (BACS) to Hearthstone Housing
Foundation (Hearthstone); and
2. Approving BACS’ assignment and Hearthstone’s assumption of BACS’ obligation
under the loan and regulatory agreements for Golden Bear Homes; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute all original or amended
documents to effectuate this action.
sections.current_situation:
Memar Properties, Inc (MPI) is seeking City approval to change the ownership
partnership of Golden Bear Homes, a City-funded Homekey project. Golden Bear
Homes, LP is currently made up of MPI (as the administrative general partner) and
BACS (as the Managing General Partner), with MPI providing property management
and operational support, and BACS providing compliance oversight and supportive
services to residents. BACS is interested in increasing organizational capacity for other
existing and upcoming projects and intends to exit the partnership.
MPI identified Hearthstone as their preferred replacement partner. Hearthstone is a
nonprofit organization that has operated since 1992. It has managed a total of 270
rental projects in its organizational lifetime. Hearthstone’s current portfolio includes 177
low-income rental housing projects, including many Homekey projects that serve
Extremely Low-Income households. The majority serve formerly homeless households,
people with disabilities, or senior households. Hearthstone currently manages 11 rental
projects in the Greater Bay Area and 3 rental projects in Alameda County, one of which
is a Homekey project.
Hearthstone will be responsible for securing welfare exemption from the County
annually and overseeing compliance with all regulatory agreements. Additionally, on-
site wraparound supportive services for the 43 formerly homeless individuals, previously

sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no expected fiscal impacts. The City entered into a loan agreement with
Golden Bear Homes, LP for $8.46 million in capital and operations subsidy funding to
support the project. Currently, there are no expected additions to this loan.
sections.background:
On December 14, 2021, Council approved three resolutions (70,136- N.S., 70,137-
N.S., and 70,138 – N.S.) reserving $8,463,535 in affordable housing funds for the
Golden Bear Homes permanent supportive housing project at 1620 San Pablo as
proposed by a partnership between MPI and BACS. These resolutions also authorized
City staff to take actions needed for the City’s participation in the State of California’s
HCD Homekey program, including entering into standard agreement with the HCD. The
reservation described came from City affordable housing funds, including general funds
received pursuant to Measure P, and Permanent Local Housing Allocation funds.
Golden Bear Homes, LP completed an initial phase of rehab focused on accessibility
upgrades in December 2022 and achieved full occupancy in January 2023. The team
recently completed the second and final phase of construction work, including
landscaping and installation of a modular unit with community and services space.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There will be no environmental impacts related to the transfer, and the project will
remain as housing for homeless adults.
sections.rationale:
Golden Bear Homes, in its current configuration, is no longer compatible with BACS’
organizational priorities. Transferring the Managing General Partner role to
Hearthstone, whose mission includes prioritizing housing and providing supportive
services to homeless, will support the ongoing operation of Golden Bear Homes as
permanent supportive housing for formerly homeless adults.

Page 3 of 4
Golden Bear Homes Partnership Transfer CONSENT CALENDAR
November 18, 2025
sections.alternatives:
None considered.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AUTHORIZING THE OWNERSHIP TRANSFER OF GOLDEN BEAR HOMES, LP
FROM BAY AREA COMMUNITY SERVICES TO HEARTHSTONE HOUSING
FOUNDATION
WHEREAS, in 2021, the City of Berkeley (City) provided a development loan to Golden
Bear Homes, LP to acquire and rehabilitate Golden Bear Homes, located at 1620 San
Pablo Ave, Berkeley, CA, and operate the property as permanent supportive housing for
formerly homeless individuals; and
WHEREAS, Golden Bear Homes, LP is comprised of Memar Properties, Inc. (MPI) as the
administrative general partner providing property management and operational support
and Bay Area Community Services (BACS) as the Managing General Partner providing
compliance oversight and supportive services; and
WHEREAS, BACS is interested in increasing its organizational capacity for existing and
upcoming projects and wishes to exit Golden Bear Homes, LP; and
WHEREAS, in early 2025, MPI began exploring the option to transfer the Managing
General Partner role of Golden Bear Homes to another entity with the experience and
mission to provide housing with supportive services to extremely low-income households;
and
WHEREAS, in July 2025, MPI submitted a letter to the City requesting transfer of the
Managing General Partner for Golden Bear Homes, LP from BACS to Hearthstone
Housing Foundation (Hearthstone) with supportive services provided by Housing
Consortium of the East Bay; and
WHEREAS, the City’s existing Development Loan Agreement with Golden Bear Homes,
LP requires City approval of any transfers, including partnership transfers.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City approves the transfer of the Managing General Partner role for Golden Bear Homes,
LP from BACS to Hearthstone Housing Foundation.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Council approves BACS’ assignment and
Hearthstone Housing Foundation’s assumption of BACS’ obligations under the loan
agreement and regulatory agre
